seoTitle: Duverney vs Eagle Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Duverney and Eagle Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Duverney vs Eagle Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Duverney and Eagle Point.
heading: Duverney vs Eagle Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Duverney, Victoria and Eagle Point, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Duverney has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Eagle Point (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Duverney or Eagle Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Duverney has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Eagle Point scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Duverney scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Eagle Point scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Duverney or Eagle Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Duverney has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
